text: oh so THAT is why people say state is the heartbeat of a component
it is all fun and games until you start prop drilling into oblivion
wait i thought state was just variables that react to change
technically yes but the trigger for re-renders is where people usually trip up
the real trap is trying to store everything in state just because it changes
if it does not affect the ui do not put it in state
lol i have definitely done that with form refs before
wait so why use refs then
refs are for when you need to access the dom directly or persist a value without triggering a render
it is a massive headache when you are first starting out
yeah i still struggle with when to lift state up vs just context
